memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Greene, Green and others. First found in Kent, where the Greene family held a family seat from very early times. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Aderton Greene, who came to Virginia in 1623; John Greene, who settled in Boston in 1625; Abigail Greene, who arrived in Boston, Massachusetts in 1632. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009) Motto Translated: Virtue is always flourishing. Recorded in the spellings of Green and Greene, this is one of the most widespread of English, and sometimes Irish, surnames. It is usually of pre-7th century origins, and derives from the word "grene" meaning green. As such it may be topographical for a person resident by a village green or even a place called Green, or as a status name for a young man who played the part of the mystic and fertile "Green Man" sometimes known as "Jack in the Green", in the May Day fertility celebrations. In this context "green" was symbolic of youthful ardor, spring, and the re-growth of nature. Sometimes the surname can be of Irish origins, and a translation of the ancient Gaelic given name "Uaithne". As this also means "green," it probably has the same basic meaning and origin as the English form. Examples of the early recordings taken from authentic rolls, registers and charters of the Middle Ages, include Richard de la Grene of the county of Norfolk in the year 1200; William Grene in the Hundred Rolls of Yorkshire in 1230; Robert Othegreen, and Henry on the Green, both of Worcestershire, in 1274. Among the many distinguished name bearers listed in the "Dictionary of National Biography" are Charles Green (1785 - 1870), an early aeronaut, who made the first ascent with a hydrogen gas balloon in 1821. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Geoffrey de Grene. This was dated 1188, in the "Pipe Rolls" of the county of Kent", during the reign of King Henry 2nd of England. He was known as "The Builder of Churches". 1154 - 1189. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was sometimes known as the Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Bennet Greene於1552年5月17日出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，她的父親Lawrence Greene28歲，母親Margaret Spencer16歲。她於1589年7月1日與John Wright在英國英格蘭艾可凱爾維頓 哈契結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1590年11月4日在英國米德萊可享年38歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可素斯 維亞爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Bennet Greene于1552年5月17日出生在英国米德萊可倫敦时，她的父亲Lawrence Greene28岁，母亲Margaret Spencer16岁。她于1589年7月1日与John Wright在英国英格兰艾可凱爾維頓 哈契结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1590年11月4日在英国米德萊可享年38岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可素斯 維亞爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Greene
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Green  . In North America, this surname has assimilated cognates from other languages.

variants.name: Green
meanings: English: either a nickname for someone who was fond of dressing in this color (Old English grēne) or was young or immature, or who had played the part of the ‘Green Man’ in the May Day celebrations, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a village green (Middle English grene, a transferred use of the color term). This is one of the most common and widespread of English surnames. In North America it has assimilated cognates from other languages, notably German Grün (see Gruen ) and Dutch Groen ; compare 7 below. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: alternatively, from a Middle English personal name Grene.
Irish: adopted for Ó hUainín ‘descendant of Uainín’, a personal name from a pet form of uaine ‘green’, see Honan .

nameMeanings.name: Bennet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The normal medieval vernacular form of   Benedict  , borne by women as well as men in the past. Now sometimes used as an antiquarian revival, but more often a transferred use of the surname derived from the medieval given name.

variants.name: Benett
meanings: The normal medieval vernacular form of   Benedict  , borne by women as well as men in the past. Now sometimes used as an antiquarian revival, but more often a transferred use of the surname derived from the medieval given name.

variants.name: Benet
meanings: From Church Latin benedictus ‘blessed’. This was the name of the saint ( c.480– c.550 ) who composed the Benedictine rule of Christian monastic life that is still followed in essence by all Western orders. He was born near Spoleto in Umbria, central Italy. After studying in Rome, he went to live as a hermit at Subiaco, and later organized groups of followers and imitators into monastic cells. In c.529 he moved to Monte Cassino, where he founded the great monastery that is still the centre of the Benedictine order. His rule is simple, restrained, and practical. See also Bennett .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Bennet Greene was born on 17 May 1552, in London, Middlesex, England, her father, Lawrence Greene, was 28 and her mother, Margaret Spencer, was 16. She married John Wright on 1 July 1589, in Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 4 November 1590, in Middlesex, England, at the age of 38, and was buried in South Weald, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Bennet Greene est née le 17 mai 1552, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Lawrence Greene, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Margaret Spencer, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé John Wright le 1 juillet 1589, à Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 4 novembre 1590, à Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterrée à South Weald, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Bennet Greeneが1552年5月17日にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで生まれた時，父Lawrence Greeneは28歳，母Margaret Spencerは16歳であった。1589年7月1日にイギリスイギリスエセックスケルベドン ハトチでJohn Wrightと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1590年11月4日にイギリスミッドレセックスで38歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスソウス ウエアルドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Bennet Greene(이)가 1552년 5월 17일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lawrence Greene(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Spencer(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1589년 7월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 켈브던 해트치에서 John Wright(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1590년 11월 4일 영국 미들레세스에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 소우스 위드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bennet Greene родилась 17 мая 1552 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Lawrence Greene было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Spencer, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Wright 1 июля 1589 г. в местности келведон хач, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 4 ноября 1590 г. в местности миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 38 лет и была похоронена в местности соут вилд, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Bennet Greene nació el 17 de mayo de 1552, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Lawrence Greene, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Margaret Spencer, tenía 16 años. Se casó con John Wright el 1 de julio de 1589, en Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 4 de noviembre de 1590, en Middlesex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultada en South Weald, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
